item.title: Fed and BOE Stay Guarded After 100 Days of Iran War
item.description: For several global central banks, the question of whether the Iran war poses more of an immediate danger to inflation or to growth is likely to remain open in the coming week.

item.title: CLO ETFs Boom on Higher Rates, Private Debt Woes
item.description: Wall Street has an answer for retail investors seeking to profit from elevated interest rates and dodge defaults in private credit: funds that buy collateralized loan obligations.

item.title: Trump to Meet With Mideast Partners on Sidelines of G7 in France
item.description: President Donald Trump will meet with US partners in the Middle East at the Group of Seven leaders’ summit in France next week, underscoring the outsized role the war in Iran continues to play as European allies grapple with the global economic fallout.

item.title: US Wants to Avoid Congressional Vote on Trade Deal, Carney Says
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney said Trump administration officials have been clear that they don’t want to trigger a vote in Congress by changing the “fundamental architecture” of the North American free trade deal.

item.title: How Kalshi’s Lopes Lara Turned Kylie Jenner Gossip Into Billions
item.description: The firm’s co-founder talks about the celebrity buzz that sparked her interest in prediction markets, the legal battles her firm faces and Jay-Z’s advice.

item.title: US’s Screwworm Fix Is Still a Year Away, Risking More Spread
item.description: The US’s best weapon against a deadly cattle parasite threatening the beef industry is more than a year away from showing meaningful results, raising concerns over how far the outbreak could spread before then.

item.title: Brazil’s World Cup Grilling Tradition Challenged by Beef Prices
item.description: Soaring prices for the world’s largest beef producer, Brazil, mean households will be buying less red meat when they gather to watch Brazil’s first game of the FIFA World Cup this Saturday.

item.title: We’re All on Starship Elon Now
item.description: SpaceX is coming to a mutual fund or 401(k) near you.

item.title: Google Search Gets AI Makeover, and the World Cup Comes to North America
item.description: It’s been a big week for Google and the World Cup.

item.title: There’s a Bug in the Gold Trade as Miners Move Like Meme Stocks
item.description: Brian Laks has been investing in gold stocks for over a decade. To the deep-value investor, they serve as a hedge against volatility in the rest of his portfolio, a haven during geopolitical uncertainty and deliver steady returns.

item.title: Private Markets Titans Fight Back in Court of Public Opinion
item.description: Private capital’s problems are more perception than reality.

item.title: Global Capitalism Bets It All on AI Future That Alarms Voters
item.description: Days after filing confidentially to go public, Anthropic, the $965 billion artificial intelligence juggernaut that’s one of the fastest-growing startups of all time, dropped another bombshell.

item.title: US AI Boom Is Stoking Asia’s ‘Super’ Trade Surpluses
item.description: And much of that money may return to America in the form of direct investment.

item.title: Top Motorcycle Exporter China Urges Industry to Curb Price Wars
item.description: China’s motorcycle industry trade body urged companies across the sector to curb price wars and focus on higher-quality growth, warning that imitation and a flood of similar products are squeezing profits and hurting the reputation of Chinese manufacturing.

item.title: Poland to End Fuel Subsidies as US-Iran Peace Talks Heat Up
item.description: Poland is preparing to restore regular tax rates on fuels as peace talks between the US and Iran progress, Prime Minister Donald Tusk said at a press conference on Saturday.

item.title: Former ECB Rate-Setter Muller Reports for Duty as Volunteer Cop
item.description: Just days after finishing a seven-year stint at the helm of Estonia’s central bank, Madis Muller is preparing for a very different role: volunteering in the police’s criminal-investigations unit.

item.title: Charting the Global Economy: War Pushes Up Prices, Dents Growth
item.description: The impact of the Iran war continues to ripple across the world economy, pushing up prices and denting the outlook for growth.

item.title: Prices Likely to Stay Higher Even If Conflict Ends, Nagel Says
item.description: Prices are likely to stay elevated for longer even if the war in Iran were to end soon, Bundesbank President Joachim Nagel said in an interview with Deutschlandfunk.

item.title: Anjney Midha's Plan to Radically Lower the Price of Compute
item.description: A conversation with the founder of AMP PBC about turning GPUs into a utility.

item.title: Odd Lots: Midha’s Plan to Lower the Price of Compute (Podcast)
item.description: Odd Lots: Midha’s Plan to Lower the Price of Compute (Podcast)【原文：Odd Lots: Midha’s Plan to Lower the Price of Compute (Podcast)】

item.title: Switzerland Asks If 10 Million People Is Where to Draw the Line
item.description: A Swiss plebiscite this weekend on whether to impose a population ceiling is offering voters a choice that threatens to become their most consequential yet of the current century.

item.title: US Strikes Iranian Drones While Talks Continue for Interim Deal
item.description: The US shot down Iranian drones near the Strait of Hormuz as talks continued for an interim peace deal to reopen the strategic waterway, adding to uncertainty over when an agreement can be reached.

item.title: Cape Town Tests Johannesburg’s Hold on South African Economy
item.description: Confidence in Cape Town is being written in its skyline.

item.title: Korean Media Giant JoongAng’s JTBC Defaults, Downgraded to Junk
item.description: JTBC Co. Ltd., the broadcaster owned by South Korea’s media giant JoongAng Group, defaulted on securitized loans and was downgraded to junk by a local ratings firm, triggering a series of rating cuts across the conglomerate.

item.title: Geely to Shut Some Units, Focus on HK-Listed Arm, Chairman Says
item.description: China’s Zhejiang Geely Holding Group will streamline its structure by shutting down or merging some entities and concentrate resources around its Hong Kong-listed arm, in a bid to improve governance, Chairman Li Shufu said.

item.title: China Rebukes US Over ‘Military’ Labeling of Its Top Firms
item.description: China’s Commerce Ministry said it firmly opposes the US decision to label additional firms as “military companies,” accusing Washington of using national security as a pretext to curb the development of Chinese firms.

item.title: Why Musk Raced to Take SpaceX Public in the World’s Biggest IPO
item.description: It was meant to happen once humans were regularly flying to Mars. Then the AI boom took off.

item.title: US Kills Tren de Aragua Leader Guerrero in Strike on Compound
item.description: The US killed the leader of the Tren De Aragua drug cartel, Niño Guerrero, in a strike that was carried out with the assistance of Venezuela, President Donald Trump said.

item.title: OpenAI Probed by Coalition of State Attorneys General
item.description: OpenAI is under investigation by a coalition of state attorneys general who requested information from the artificial intelligence company on a wide range of topics.

item.title: CFTC Considers Blocking CME’s 24/7 Oil Contract Bid
item.description: The US Commodity Futures Trading Commission is considering whether to block CME Group Inc.’s bid to launch a round-the-clock oil contract, heightening tensions between the market stalwart and its regulator.
